puzzle.title: The Plea from the Depths
puzzle.intro: A divine messenger is sent to guide a bustling city, but this soul departs in anger when the message is initially ignored. This hasty departure leads to a perilous journey at sea, culminating in an unimaginable confinement. From the deepest darkness, a powerful prayer of repentance is uttered, one that pierces the gloom and changes a nation's destiny. Can you identify this figure whose story is a profound lesson in patience and Allah's boundless mercy?
clues: I was sent as a warner to the people of a great city, but they turned away from my call.
Overcome with frustration, I abandoned my mission, thinking I could flee from my Lord's decree.
I boarded a laden ship, but a mighty storm arose, and the sailors cast lots to identify the one responsible.
The lot fell upon me repeatedly, and as a result, I was thrown into the tumultuous sea.
By Allah's command, a great fish swallowed me, and I was enveloped in layers of darkness within its belly.
It was from this darkness that I called out my prayer of repentance: 'There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.'

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Yunus
Prophet Khidr
Prophet Dhul-Kifl
Prophet Ilyas
Prophet Al-Yasa

trial.label: What Was My Trial?
options: Being thrown into a fire
Building a great ark to survive a flood
Being swallowed by a great fish
Losing all wealth and family to affliction
Being imprisoned on a false accusation
trial.answer: Being swallowed by a great fish

location.label: Key Element
options: The Valley of Tuwa
The City of Iram
The belly of the fish
A cave of refuge
The shade of a gourd plant

outcome.label: What Was The Outcome?
options: My people were destroyed by a mighty blast
I was raised to the heavens alive
My people believed and their punishment was lifted
I was given a kingdom that no one after me would have
My wife was destroyed along with the disbelievers
outcome.answer: My people believed and their punishment was lifted

puzzle.arabic: فَلَوْلَا كَانَتْ قَرْيَةٌ ءَامَنَتْ فَنَفَعَهَآ إِيمَـٰنُهَآ إِلَّا قَوْمَ يُونُسَ لَمَّآ ءَامَنُوا۟ كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْخِزْىِ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَمَتَّعْنَـٰهُمْ إِلَىٰ حِينٍۢ
puzzle.verse: Then has there not been a [single] city that believed so its faith benefited it except the people of Jonah? When they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in worldly life and gave them enjoyment [i.e., provision] for a time. (10:98)
